description:
You are an urban planner preparing a building stock assessment report for San Francisco. Your goal is to analyze the age distribution of the city's building stock by zone, identify areas with the oldest infrastructure, and produce publication-ready outputs using the San Francisco UrbanSim dataset (HDF5 file at ../data/sanfran_public.h5).

Create a well-organized Jupyter notebook at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/notebooks/building_vintage_analysis.ipynb that joins the 'buildings' and 'parcels' tables to associate buildings with zones. Analyze building vintage cohorts (Pre-1940, 1940-1960, 1960-1980, 1980-2000, and 2000+), excluding records with missing or zero year_built values. For each zone, calculate the total building count, the count and percentage of buildings in each cohort, and the median year built. 

Identify the top 10 zones with the highest percentage of pre-1940 buildings among zones with at least 20 buildings. Export these top 10 zones, sorted by pre_1940_pct in descending order, to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/aging_building_zones.csv with columns: zone_id, total_buildings, pre_1940_count, pre_1940_pct, and median_year_built. Additionally, generate a stacked bar chart showing the vintage cohort distribution for these top 10 zones and save it to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/building_vintage_chart.png with a descriptive title, axis labels, and a legend. Ensure all notebook cells are executed.
